items.title: FC Groningen beëindigt vloek in Heerenveen, maar verliest Vaessen
items.summary: Net als eerder dit seizoen is de Derby van het Noorden een prooi voor FC Groningen. De ploeg van Dick Lukkien won voor het eerst in tien jaar op bezoek bij SC Heerenveen, maar verloor doelman Etienne Vaessen met een voetblessure.

items.title: Wolves v Newcastle United: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 2pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Tables | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail John Rob Edwards spoke to the BBC about the Wolves revival: “It’s still early days but performances have steadily been getting better. It’s nice now that has been backed with results. Results feed confidence and belief.” For Wolves, no Jorgen Strand Larsen in the starting lineup despite his hat-trick against Shrewsbury, and Andre into the team instead of Jhon Arias is the one change from the draw with Everton. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Volendam en Almelo leven toe naar aftrap
items.summary: Zondag is het Derby Day in de Eredivisie. We trappen af met de Derby van het Noorden tussen SC Heerenveen en FC Groningen, vervolgen met de Twentse Derby Heracles Almelo - FC Twente en sluiten af met het treffen tussen Feyenoord en Sparta Rotterdam, dat in een apart liveblog wordt behandeld. Verder is er ook buitenlands voetbal. Volg het hier in de VI Live.

items.title: 'Chelsea akkoord met Franse verdediger, maar wil geen 65 miljoen betalen'
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Ingeving Braken helpt MVV langs FC Den Bosch
items.summary: FC Den Bosch heeft zondag dure punten laten liggen in de strijd om een plek in de play-offs om promotie en degradatie. De ploeg van Ulrich Landvreugd ging ten onder tegen MVV. Sven Braken was de grote man.

items.title: Elvira snatches Dubai title after Lowry's late blunder and McIlroy's charge
items.summary: Nacho Elvira capitalised on a final-hole blunder from Shane Lowry and Sunday charge from Rory McIlroy to claim a dramatic victory at the DP World Tour's Dubai Invitational.

items.title: Martínez nodigt kritische United-iconen bij hem thuis uit, Scholes reageert gevat
items.summary: Manchester United veegde zaterdag in de stadsderby de vloer aan met Manchester City. Dat hadden United-iconen Nicky Butt en Paul Scholes niet bepaald voorspeld: zij dreven in aanloop naar de wedstrijd de spot met United-verdediger Lisandro Martínez. De Argentijn slaat terug na de ruime zege, Scholes reageert gevat.

items.title: Crystal Palace decide not to sack manager Oliver Glasner after meeting
items.summary: Steve Parish held talks with Austrian on Sunday morning Manager said board had ‘abandoned’ him on Saturday Crystal Palace have decided against sacking Oliver Glasner despite him accusing the club’s board of abandoning him and the rest of his squad by selling captain Marc Guéhi to Manchester City 24 hours before they were due to face Sunderland. Glasner’s outburst following the 2-1 defeat, when the Austrian also admitted he doesn’t care if he sees out his contract until the end of the season having already confirmed he will leave Selhurst Park, is understood to have dismayed Palace’s chair, Steve Parish. He was said to be weighing up if there was any other option than parting company with the manager who led Palace to their first major trophy when they won the FA Cup only eight months ago after Glasner went public over his dissatisfaction in two extraordinary press conferences. Continue reading...

items.title: 'He can come to my house' - Martinez hits back at Scholes criticism
items.summary: Lisandro Martinez has hit back at Paul Scholes and invited him to his house for talks after the former Man Utd midfielder criticised the defender ahead of the Manchester derby.

items.title: Utd's Martinez hits back over 'toddler' criticism
items.summary: Lisandro Martínez has challenged Paul Scholes and Nicky Butt to repeat their criticism to his face after the pair said Erling Haaland would make the Manchester United defender look like "a little toddler" in the Manchester derby.

items.title: Sources: PSG finalizing deal for Barcelona starlet
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain are finalising the remaining details for the transfer of Dro Fernández, the latest prodigy from Barcelona's academy, sources confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal denied clear penalty in Forest draw
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has said Arsenal were denied a "very clear penalty" in Saturday's 0-0 draw at Nottingham Forest.

items.title: Wrexham captain McClean exits, joins Derry City
items.summary: Wrexham have announced that their club captain James McLean has moved to Derry City.

items.title: Palace want Glasner to stay despite outburst
items.summary: Crystal Palace want Oliver Glasner to see out his contract until the end of the season despite his outburst on Saturday.

items.title: Raducanu makes winning start at Australian Open with clinical performance
items.summary: Emma Raducanu sealed a fine first-round win at the Australian Open by defeating Thailand's world No 195 Mananchaya Sawangkaew on opening Sunday.

items.title: 'No-one says anything in your face' - Martinez hits back at Scholes and Butt
items.summary: Pundits discussing Man Utd "can talk on the television" but "no-one says anything" to your face, according to defender Lisandro Martinez.

items.title: Lisandro Martínez hits back at Scholes and Manchester United punditocracy
items.summary: Former players Scholes and Butt critical before derby ‘If he wants to say something … he can come to my house’ Lisandro Martínez has criticised Paul Scholes for mocking him on a podcast but not directly to the Manchester United defender’s face, following Saturday’s 2-0 win over Manchester City at Old Trafford. Scholes and Nicky Butt, another prominent former United player, were each scathing about the diminutive Martínez and his ability to mark Erling Haaland, when speaking on the Good, the Bad and the Ugly before the 198th derby. Continue reading...

items.title: Growing sense of embarrassment at Fifa over Donald Trump peace prize
items.summary: Mid-level and senior officials uncomfortable with award Fifa says it still ‘strongly’ supports the peace prize There is a growing sense of embarrassment among mid-level and senior officials within Fifa over the awarding of its peace prize to Donald Trump. The US president was handed the award at the World Cup draw in Washington DC in December with the Fifa president, Gianni Infantino, telling Trump: “We want to see hope, we want to see unity, we want to see a future. This is what we want to see from a leader and you definitely deserve the first Fifa Peace Prize.” Since then, the US has launched airstrikes across Venezuela and captured the country’s president, Nicolás Maduro, and his wife, Cilia Flores, and flown them to the US, where he was put in jail. Maduro appeared in court on 5 January, pleading not guilty to drugs, weapons and “narco-terrorism” charges. Trump has also threatened to invade Greenland because he said the US needs the territory “very badly”. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘Would you believe it?!’ | Lowry's title hopes ended after water nightmare
items.summary: Shane Lowry finds the water off this bunker shot on the last hole of the Dubai Invitational.

items.title: Spurs chiefs consider sacking Frank as fans turn
items.summary: Tottenham head coach Thomas Frank is under significant internal scrutiny with the club considering calling time on his seven-month reign.
